items.title: Medical Officers Are Slaves Too — MO In Internal Medicine Department
items.summary: Besides housemen, medical officers are slaves too. MOs clerk patients, formulate the management plan, stabilise patients, and initiate investigations. The same MO continues to run the ward service. One MO may be responsible for 30 to 40 patients at a time. The post Medical Officers Are Slaves Too — MO In Internal Medicine Department appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Time To Change Our Antiquated Public Health Care System — Dr DoGood
items.summary: In many health care systems, blood-taking has been routinely performed by phlebotomists since the 90s, but this work frequently falls to junior doctors in Malaysia's public hospitals. Clarion calls for health care reform have fallen onto deaf ears. The post Time To Change Our Antiquated Public Health Care System — Dr DoGood appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Singapore Agents Poaching Malaysian Medical Students Before Graduation
items.summary: Health Minister Dzulkefly Ahmad says Singapore recruiters are approaching Malaysian medical students, even before they sit for their final exams, with lucrative pay and permanent jobs. He pledges to end Malaysia’s contract doctor system. The post Singapore Agents Poaching Malaysian Medical Students Before Graduation appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Dzulkefly: ‘360-Degree’ Information Needed Before Confirming Bullying In Kuala Krai Doctor’s Death
items.summary: Dr Dzul says "360-degree" information is needed before deciding if bullying had led to the death of a Kuala Krai houseman. The health minister also tells HODs and hospital directors not to use house officers as an "extra pair of hands" for daily chores. The post Dzulkefly: ‘360-Degree’ Information Needed Before Confirming Bullying In Kuala Krai Doctor’s Death appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Government Studying Pig Farming Industry Reform To Secure Pork Supply: Minister
items.summary: KPKM Minister Mat Sabu says the government is studying Malaysia’s pig farming industry to strengthen policy and sustain pork supply. The review comes as pork self-sufficiency has fallen below 70 per cent and amid debate over pig farming bans in Selangor. The post Government Studying Pig Farming Industry Reform To Secure Pork Supply: Minister appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Living With Prostate Cancer: The Hidden Burden On Family Caregivers
items.summary: Living with prostate cancer is a journey that affects the entire family. With the right information, support, and medical guidance, caregivers can play a powerful role in helping their loved ones navigate treatment and daily life with dignity and confidence. The post Living With Prostate Cancer: The Hidden Burden On Family Caregivers appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Hold HOD, Hospital Director Accountable When Doctors Die — Excommunicado
items.summary: If a doctor dies in a department where there is bullying, repeated shouting, public humiliation, fear, silence, unbearable workload, or ignored complaints, then the head of department (HOD) and the hospital director must be held responsible. The post Hold HOD, Hospital Director Accountable When Doctors Die — Excommunicado appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: When Fellowship Guidelines Are Ignored, Who Is Accountable? — Physician
items.summary: MOH must clarify if a 2-year waiting period to apply for fellowship or subspecialty training remains official policy. Recently, physicians who didn't complete the 2-year waiting period requirement were accepted into internal medicine subspecialty training. The post When Fellowship Guidelines Are Ignored, Who Is Accountable? — Physician appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: From Silent Disease To Systemic Risk: Why Kidney Health Matters More Than We Think
items.summary: Chronic kidney disease is becoming one of Malaysia’s most pressing public health challenges, yet it often develops quietly and goes unnoticed until it reaches an advanced stage. The post From Silent Disease To Systemic Risk: Why Kidney Health Matters More Than We Think appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Nurse Migration: Can Malaysia Afford To Lose Its Caregivers? — Nadira Amalina Azizan & Wong Lee Sia
items.summary: Policymakers, health care leaders, and society at large must recognise that retaining nurses is not a cost, but an investment in public health security. items.title: Sabah DIDR faces multiple graft charges in special corruption court
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah Industrial Development and Research Department (DIDR) director Tseu Kei Yue, has been charged with 20 counts of abetment of his staff in corruption-related offences at the Kota Kinabalu Special Corruption Court. Read full story

items.summary: KUCHING: Sarawak's medical tourism sector is poised to expand after recording positive growth in the last two years. Read full story
